scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a cold-chain-specific handoff package with explicit ownership, lot-aware artifacts, reefer and logger context, and blocker state.
Missing lot, destination-acceptance, logger, and release prerequisites were treated as blockers rather than a routable handoff.
Duplicate ownership and conflicting dock or trailer schedules were escalated with an owner map instead of dropped into generic coordination.
Conflicting ERP, MES, WMS, TMS, and logger records were preserved as a traceability blocker and not collapsed into a confident answer.
Quality-hold posture was preserved and formal excursion disposition and release authority stayed with quality owners.
Supplier and substitute-material approval was rejected and routed to supplier-quality and change-control owners.
The lane refused to absorb trailer-release, dock, warehouse-move, and case-closure execution work and returned boundary-safe routing guidance.
Missing tenant temperature specs, excursion thresholds, and owner maps correctly triggered a low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting public and tenant excursion-disposition rules were escalated for human resolution instead of synthesized into a definitive instruction.
Cold-chain terms were normalized into authoritative artifact and status states before routing.
Closure was only confirmed after destination-owner proof and required artifact completeness were explicit.
Supplier or carrier temperature nonconformance was escalated with throughput risk preserved and without unsupported continuation.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to temperature-controlled manufacturing coordination, logger and traceability evidence, hold boundaries, and owner routing.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and all manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities required by the issue contract.
